message: 🌱 Add harbor: Support for Redis TLS mission (#362)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "harbor-13223-support-for-redis-tls",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "harbor: Support for Redis TLS",
+    "description": "Support for Redis TLS. This issue affects 38+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify harbor feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=harbor\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: Support for Redis TLS"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant harbor resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get resource -A\nkubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>\n```\nAs a Harbor instance operator, i want to secure the traffic to Redis instance.\n\nThis issue might seem not so pressing when internal Redis is used. However, external managed Redis instances can be used"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for Redis TLS",
+        "description": "Add HA Harbor deployment, utilizing Postgres and Redis operators for external databases. Images and charts are stored in the Swift object storage. Installation steps are also added as well as instructions on how Fluxcd can be used.\n```yaml\nexternal:\r\n    # support redis, redis+sentinel\r\n    # addr for redis: <host_redis>:<port_redis>\r\n    # addr for redis+sentinel: <host_sentinel1>:<port_sentinel1>,<host_sentinel2>:<port_sentinel2>,<host_sentinel3>:<port_sentinel3>\r\n    addr: \"192.168.0.2:6379\"\r\n    # The name of the set of Redis instances to monitor, it must be set to support redis+sentinel\r\n    sentinelMasterSet: \"\"\r\n    # The \"coreDatabaseIndex\" must be \"0\" as the library Harbor\r\n    # used doesn't support configuring it\r\n    # harborDatabaseIndex defaults to \"0\", but it can be configured to \"6\", this config is optional\r\n    \n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Support for Redis TLS is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: Add HA Harbor deployment, utilizing Postgres and Redis operators for external databases. Images and charts are stored in the Swift object storage. Installation steps are also added as well as instructions on how Fluxcd can be used.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "external:\r\n    # support redis, redis+sentinel\r\n    # addr for redis: <host_redis>:<port_redis>\r\n    # addr for redis+sentinel: <host_sentinel1>:<port_sentinel1>,<host_sentinel2>:<port_sentinel2>,<host_sentinel3>:<port_sentinel3>\r\n    addr: \"192.168.0.2:6379\"\r\n    # The name of the set of Redis instances to monitor, it must be set to support redis+sentinel\r\n    sentinelMasterSet: \"\"\r\n    # The \"coreDatabaseIndex\" must be \"0\" as the library Harbor\r\n    # used doesn't support configuring it\r\n    # harborDatabaseIndex defaults to \"0\", but it can be configured to \"6\", this config is optional\r\n    # cacheLayerDatabaseIndex defaults to \"0\", but it can be configured to \"7\", this config is optional\r\n    coreDatabaseIndex: \"0\"\r\n    jobserviceDatabaseIndex: \"1\"\r\n    registryDatabaseIndex: \"2\"\r\n    t"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "harbor",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",

+    ],

+    ],

+    ],

+      "issue": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/13223",

+    },

+  },
